title: Airbnb Q1 2026 Financial Results
text: Airbnb reported strong Q1 2026 results, with revenue rising 18% year-over-year to $2.7B, above the high end of guidance. Gross Booking Value reached $29.2B, up 19%. Nights and Seats Booked grew 9% to 156 million. Net income was $160M for a 6% margin, while Adjusted EBITDA increased 24% to $519M with a 19% margin. Free Cash Flow was $1.7B, a 64% margin, reflecting the cash-generative model. Management is raising its guidance for 2026 and now expects year-over-year revenue growth to accelerate to low to mid-teens, and anticipates adjusted EBITDA margin to be at least 35%. Q2 2026 Revenue Guidance is $3.54 billion-$3.60 billion, reflecting 14%-16% year-over-year growth, including a ~3% foreign exchange tailwind. Management cited conflict-related cancellations across EMEA and APAC in March, resulting in a 100-basis-point drag on nights booked and expected continued impact in upcoming periods.
